task_completion.description: Verify the baseline H-Rotor VAWT configuration is set up correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade rotor design interface showing a VAWT with 3 blades, Radius=2m, Blade Height=4m, Chord=0.25m, and using the NACA 0018 airfoil.

task_completion.description: Verify the strutted VAWT configuration is set up correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade rotor design interface showing struts added to the rotor (2 struts per blade, Strut Chord=0.05m, Strut Cd=1.2).

task_completion.description: Verify DMS simulations were executed for the required parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade simulation module showing DMS simulation results (e.g., Cp vs TSR graphs or tables) covering TSR 1 to 6 with a step of 0.5, at a wind speed of 12m/s.

task_completion.description: Verify the QBlade project was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade window title bar showing 'vawt_strut_study.wpa' or a file manager window showing the file 'vawt_strut_study.wpa' in the /home/ga/Documents/projects/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the text report file was created at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the file 'strut_loss_report.txt' located in /home/ga/Documents/projects/.

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains the required metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'strut_loss_report.txt' explicitly listing three values: Baseline Max Cp, Strutted Max Cp, and the Percentage of Cp Reduction.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used QBlade to perform the simulations rather than fabricating the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the QBlade GUI with the specific rotor models and simulation graphs generated, not just a text editor or terminal with guessed values.

integrity.description: Ensure the reported Cp values match the simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Baseline Max Cp and Strutted Max Cp values written in the text report should correspond to the peak values visible on the Cp vs TSR graphs or tables in QBlade.

integrity.description: Ensure the percentage reduction is calculated correctly from the reported values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Percentage of Cp Reduction' in the report must be mathematically correct based on the formula: ((Baseline Max Cp - Strutted Max Cp) / Baseline Max Cp) * 100.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/projects/vawt_strut_study.wpa and the report to /home/ga/Documents/projects/strut_loss_report.txt. A realistic Baseline Max Cp for this specific H-rotor configuration is between 0.30 and 0.55. The strut drag typically causes a Cp reduction between 1.0% and 40.0%. If the reported baseline Cp is outside 0.30-0.55, the simulation was likely set up incorrectly.
